posts.text:
The cost of buying and owning a car has soared since the pandemic. 

Navy Federal's Cost of Car Ownership Index is +41% since January 2020
CPI is +26% since January 2020
Wages are +31%

Almost every aspect of buying, owning, insuring and repairing a car has jumped. And now we are in the midst of another gas price shock. 

‘It’s Just Crazy’: High Car Payments Make Ownership Feel Impossible by @onlykailyn 
nytimes.com/2026/03/16/busin…

posts.text:
Good to see this coordinated move to release 400 million barrels of oil around the world.

It will help.
But it's unlikely to cover the full loss of ~11 million to 16 million barrels of daily loss right now without the Strait of Hormuz operating.


Phil Stewart (@phildstewart)

(Reuters) - The International Energy Agency (IEA) proposed on Wednesday the release of 400 million barrels of oil, the largest release of oil reserves in its history, to bring down crude prices that have soared amid the U

posts.text:
The biggest problem for many Americans is that we have "whack-a-mole" inflation. Just as some items get back to more normal price points, other items start spiking. It's hard to budget and plan when you don't know what's spiking next.

Look at the February data.
Overall 2.4% inflation isn't bad.
But...
Restaurants +3.9%
Medical care +4.1%
Electricity +4.8%
Utility gas +10.9%

And we all know gas prices spiked since this data was collected. It's hard for moderate-income Americans when food, utili
